info.title: Revert timeout export, fix test (@general subagent)

parts.text:
You are working in /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe on branch ISS-24/allow-list-connections.

## Task
Revert the export of `URLFetchTimeout` back to `urlFetchTimeout` (unexported). The integration test should not need to override internal package state. Instead, redesign the test to avoid the timeout issue entirely.

## Changes needed

### 1. Revert `internal/tool/url_fetch.go`
Change `URLFetchTimeout` back to `urlFetchTimeout` on line 23 and all references in the file (lines 23, 25, 147, 156).

### 2. Revert `internal/tool/url_fetch_test.go`
Change `URLFetchTimeout` back to `urlFetchTimeout` in the test file (lines 368-370, 402-404).

### 3. Fix `cmd/run_integration_test.go` `TestIntegration_AllowedHosts_EmptyAllowlistPermitsPublicHosts`

Remove the `tool.URLFetchTimeout` override. Instead, redesign the test:

The test's purpose is to verify that with an empty `allowed_hosts`, the url_fetch tool does NOT produce an "allowlist" error. We don't need to actually make a network connection. Use a URL with a hostname that will fail DNS resolution quickly (not a timeout). The key insight: `example.invalid` is a reserved TLD (RFC 2606) that DNS resolvers return NXDOMAIN for immediately.

Replace the test with:
```go
func TestIntegration_AllowedHosts_EmptyAllowlistPermitsPublicHosts(t *testing.T) {
	resetRunCmd(t)

	mock := testutil.NewMockLLMServer(t, []testutil.MockLLMResponse{
		testutil.AnthropicToolUseResponse("Let me fetch that.", []testutil.MockToolCall{
			{ID: "tc_uf3", Name: "url_fetch", Input: map[string]string{"url": "https://test.invalid/data"}},
		}),
		testutil.AnthropicResponse("Got a DNS error, as expected."),
	})

	configDir, _ := testutil.SetupXDGDirs(t)
	writeAgentConfig(t, configDir, "fetch-open", `name = "fetch-open"
model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
tools = ["url_fetch"]
`)

	t.Setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "test-key")
	t.Setenv("AXE_ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", mock.URL())

	buf := new(bytes.Buffer)
	rootCmd.SetOut(buf)
	rootCmd.SetErr(new(bytes.Buffer))
	rootCmd.SetArgs([]string{"run", "fetch-open"})

	err := rootCmd.Execute()
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
	}

	if mock.RequestCount() != 2 {
		t.Fatalf("expected 2 requests, got %d", mock.RequestCount())
	}

	body := mock.Requests[1].Body
	if strings.Contains(body, "not in allowed_hosts") {
		t.Errorf("empty allowlist should not produce 'not in allowed_hosts' error, got:\n%s", body)
	}
}
```

Remove any `import` of `"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool"` if it was added only for the timeout override.

## Verification
1. Run `go build ./...`
2. Run `go test -run "TestIntegration_AllowedHosts" -v ./cmd/`
3. Run `go test ./internal/tool/`
4. Run `go test ./cmd/`

Return all output.
I'll work through these changes systematically. Let me start by reading the relevant files.

parts.text:
All four verification steps pass. Here's a summary of what was done:

1. **`internal/tool/url_fetch.go`** — Reverted `URLFetchTimeout` back to `urlFetchTimeout` (unexported) at the declaration (line 23) and both usages (lines 147, 156).

2. **`internal/tool/url_fetch_test.go`** — Updated `TestURLFetch_PerRequestTimeout` and `TestURLFetch_ParentContextWinsOverPerRequestTimeout` to reference the unexported `urlFetchTimeout` (valid since the test is in `package tool`).

3. **`cmd/run_integration_test.go`** — Removed the `tool.URLFetchTimeout` override and the `"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool"` import. Replaced the URL `http://192.0.2.1:1/data` with `https://test.invalid/data` — the `.invalid` TLD (RFC 2606) guarantees an immediate NXDOMAIN from DNS, so the test gets a fast DNS error without needing to control the HTTP timeout at all.
